items.title: Middle East live: US-Iran peace talks underway as strait of Hormuz remains closed
items.standfirst: Iran said decision comes after waves of Israeli strikes in Lebanon, as delegations meet at Swiss resort for talksUS-Iran talks in Switzerland to get under way as strait of Hormuz remains closedThere ⁠was and is ‘no restriction’ on ⁠Israeli ⁠soldiers ​to act to eliminate ⁠threats in Lebanon, and that troops would not withdraw from the security zone, ‌Israeli defence minister ‌Israel Katz said in a statement on Sunday, according to Reuters.Israeli strikes killed at ‌least 20 people in Lebanon ​on Saturday, Lebanon’s state news agency NNA reported, ⁠a day after a ​ceasefire with ​Iran-backed Hezbollah ​took effect ​after ‌months of ​escalating ​violence. items.title: US-Iran talks in Switzerland get under way as strait of Hormuz remains closed
items.standfirst: JD Vance says talks aim to ‘make progress on the nuclear issue, make progress on the Lebanon ceasefire issue’Middle East crisis – live updatesTalks between Iran and the US aimed at building out the fragile interim deal to end the war have got under way in Switzerland, beset by difficulties including an Iranian decision to keep the strait of Hormuz closed in protest at Donald Trump’s inability to force Israel to end the fighting in Lebanon.The US vice-president, JD Vance, leading the US delegation, said he was adding Lebanon to an agenda that had originally been conceived to focus on the opening of the strait, the lifting of US sanctions on Iranian oil exports and the unfreezing of Iranian assets held overseas. Continue reading...
items.content: JD Vance says talks aim to ‘make progress on the nuclear issue, make progress on the Lebanon ceasefire issue’Middle East crisis – live updatesTalks between Iran and the US aimed at building out the fragile interim deal to end the war have got under way in Switzerland, beset by difficulties including an Iranian decision to keep the strait of Hormuz closed in protest at Donald Trump’s inability to force Israel to end the fighting in Lebanon.The US vice-president, JD Vance, leading the US delegation, said he was adding Lebanon to an agenda that had originally been conceived to focus on the opening of the strait, the lifting of US sanctions on Iranian oil exports and the unfreezing of Iranian assets held overseas. Continue reading...

items.content: US-Iran delegations arrive as talks begin in Switzerland.

items.title: Vance arrives in Switzerland for crunch US-Iran talks
items.standfirst: Negotiations aimed at reaching permanent end to war will initially focus on Israeli-Hizbollah conflict in Lebanon
items.content: Negotiations aimed at reaching permanent end to war will initially focus on Israeli-Hizbollah conflict in Lebanon

items.title: Israel and Hezbollah trade fire, as US-Iran talks begin in Switzerland
items.standfirst: The Iranian Revolutionary Guards declared the Strait of Hormuz shut again on Saturday. Meanwhile Iranian state media says high level officials have arrived in Switzerland for peace talks starting this Sunday. And after initially cancelling his trip, US Vice President JD Vance has landed to take part. Tehran's nuclear programme and other points in the agreement are conditional on a halt in fighting in Lebanon. But the Lebanese civil defence said 20 people were killed by Israeli strikes on Saturday. Details from the ground by FRANCE 24 correspondent in Tehran and Beirut, Reza Sayah and Renée Davis.
items.content: The Iranian Revolutionary Guards declared the Strait of Hormuz shut again on Saturday. Meanwhile Iranian state media says high level officials have arrived in Switzerland for peace talks starting this Sunday. And after initially cancelling his trip, US Vice President JD Vance has landed to take part. Tehran's nuclear programme and other points in the agreement are conditional on a halt in fighting in Lebanon. But the Lebanese civil defence said 20 people were killed by Israeli strikes on Saturday. Details from the ground by FRANCE 24 correspondent in Tehran and Beirut, Reza Sayah and Renée Davis.

items.title: Vance in Switzerland for Iran talks as Trump threatens ‘guardian angel’ toll in strait
items.standfirst: Vice President JD Vance landed in Switzerland early Sunday for talks with Iranian officials, with the days-old peace deal under threat by Israel’s strikes on Lebanon and Iran’s threats to ships in the Strait of Hormuz
items.content: Vice President JD Vance landed in Switzerland early Sunday for talks with Iranian officials, with the days-old peace deal under threat by Israel’s strikes on Lebanon and Iran’s threats to ships in the Strait of Hormuz

items.title: US-Iran peace talks to begin, after Tehran closed Strait of Hormuz
items.standfirst: Ahead of negotiations in Switzerland, The Iranian Revolutionary Guard Corps has said vessels should not approach the strait of Hormuz else their security would be at risk. Iran shut the shipping lane on Saturday, saying that Israeli strikes in Lebanon constituted a breach of the preliminary ceasefire, agreed between the US and Israel on Wednesday. Iranian and American delegations are set to meet in Switzerland on Sunday to work out their interim agreement to end the war. Story by Caroline Baum and Camille Corcoran.
items.content: Ahead of negotiations in Switzerland, The Iranian Revolutionary Guard Corps has said vessels should not approach the strait of Hormuz else their security would be at risk. Iran shut the shipping lane on Saturday, saying that Israeli strikes in Lebanon constituted a breach of the preliminary ceasefire, agreed between the US and Israel on Wednesday. Iranian and American delegations are set to meet in Switzerland on Sunday to work out their interim agreement to end the war. Story by Caroline Baum and Camille Corcoran.

items.title: US-Iran talks in Switzerland: Is Lebanon top of agenda; who is attending?

items.title: Iran says it is closing strait of Hormuz over Israeli strikes in Lebanon
items.standfirst: Unclear if threat has been carried out or if move will jeopardise talks with US scheduled for SundayMiddle East latest updatesIran has said it is closing the strait of Hormuz after waves of Israeli strikes in Lebanon in a move that threatens to derail the fragile interim peace deal with the US, signed just days ago.The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps warned ships not to approach the strategic waterway, which before the war carried a fifth of global oil and liquid gas supplies, citing what it called Israeli crimes in Lebanon and a US violation of commitments to establish a ceasefire there. Continue reading...
items.content: Unclear if threat has been carried out or if move will jeopardise talks with US scheduled for SundayMiddle East latest updatesIran has said it is closing the strait of Hormuz after waves of Israeli strikes in Lebanon in a move that threatens to derail the fragile interim peace deal with the US, signed just days ago.The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps warned ships not to approach the strategic waterway, which before the war carried a fifth of global oil and liquid gas supplies, citing what it called Israeli crimes in Lebanon and a US violation of commitments to establish a ceasefire there. items.standfirst: The US disputed Iran's claim the waterway is shut, a move Tehran says was a response to Israeli attacks in Lebanon.

items.standfirst: Vice President JD Vance heads to Switzerland for peace talks with Iran; President Trump defends his beautification push in Washington, D.C.
items.content: Vice President JD Vance heads to Switzerland for peace talks with Iran; President Trump defends his beautification push in Washington, D.C.

items.title: Trump defends beautification push in D.C.
items.standfirst: President Trump's push to renovate landmarks in Washington, D.C., ahead of America's 250th birthday is drawing criticism from critics who say the process is being conducted without the usual approvals. Taurean Small explains.
items.content: President Trump's push to renovate landmarks in Washington, D.C., ahead of America's 250th birthday is drawing criticism from critics who say the process is being conducted without the usual approvals. Taurean Small explains.

items.title: Vance heads to Switzerland for Iran peace talks
items.standfirst: Vice President JD Vance departed for Switzerland Saturday to begin direct long-term peace negotiations with Iran. His trip comes as shipping in the Strait of Hormuz faced new disruptions after Iran said it was closing the waterway. Imtiaz Tyab reports.
items.content: Vice President JD Vance departed for Switzerland Saturday to begin direct long-term peace negotiations with Iran. His trip comes as shipping in the Strait of Hormuz faced new disruptions after Iran said it was closing the waterway. Imtiaz Tyab reports.

items.title: Iran closes Strait of Hormuz over ceasefire violations, state media reports
items.standfirst: Iran said it has closed the Strait of Hormuz over ceasefire violations, citing Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon, according to state media. U.S. Central Command released a statement stating that 55 merchant ships transited the strait on Saturday.
items.content: Iran said it has closed the Strait of Hormuz over ceasefire violations, citing Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon, according to state media. U.S. Central Command released a statement stating that 55 merchant ships transited the strait on Saturday.

items.title: Vance arrives in Switzerland for peace deal talks as Iran says Strait of Hormuz is closed
items.standfirst: Iran said Saturday that the Strait of Hormuz is closed, citing ceasefire violations after Israel continued deadly strikes in southern Lebanon overnight
items.content: Iran said Saturday that the Strait of Hormuz is closed, citing ceasefire violations after Israel continued deadly strikes in southern Lebanon overnight

items.title: JD Vance arrives in Switzerland for talks with Iran over fragile peace deal
items.standfirst: JD Vance arrived in Switzerland on Sunday for talks with Iranian officials, hoping to consolidate the fragile interim deal to end the war and kickstart fresh discussions over Tehran's nuclear program.
items.content: JD Vance arrived in Switzerland on Sunday for talks with Iranian officials, hoping to consolidate the fragile interim deal to end the war and kickstart fresh discussions over Tehran's nuclear program.(Image credit: Hassan Ammar)

items.title: Al Jazeera roundly rejects Israeli accusations against journalist killed in Gaza strike
items.standfirst: Al Jazeera on Sunday roundly denied Israeli accusations that one of its journalists, killed in Gaza a day earlier, was a Hamas operative, as family and colleagues mourned the cameraman in the Palestinian territory. The Israeli military made the allegations against journalist Ahmed Wishah without supporting evidence.

bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, France24, Al Jazeera

items.title: Colombians vote in consequential presidential runoff
items.standfirst: Colombia faces a tight runoff election between a progressive and a conservative outsider.
items.content: Colombia faces a tight runoff election between a progressive and a conservative outsider.

items.title: Who is Ivan Cepeda, left-wing candidate in Colombia's presidential elections?
items.standfirst: Colombians are heading to the polls this Sunday for the second round of the presidential election. Up against the far-right Abelardo de la Espriella, the left-wing candidate Ivan Cepeda is the political heir to outgoing president Gustavo Petro. Report by Rodrigo Sedano and Aitor Chavarri, narrated by Juliette Brown.
items.content: Colombians are heading to the polls this Sunday for the second round of the presidential election. Up against the far-right Abelardo de la Espriella, the left-wing candidate Ivan Cepeda is the political heir to outgoing president Gustavo Petro. Report by Rodrigo Sedano and Aitor Chavarri, narrated by Juliette Brown.

items.title: Colombians to vote in presidential run-off pitting leftist against hardliner
items.standfirst: Run-off election sees leftist Ivan Cepeda and far-right lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella vying for the presidency.
items.content: Run-off election sees leftist Ivan Cepeda and far-right lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella vying for the presidency.

items.title: Who is Abelardo de la Esperiella, far-right candidate in the Colombian presidential election?
items.standfirst: As Colombian voters head to the polls to elect their new president, Abelardo de la Esperiella is gaining momentum. Facing the left's Ivan Cepeda, the far-right candidate promises a radical change for the country and assures voters he will restore security with an iron fist. He was promoting his rum and wine businesses before launching his presidential bid. Report by Maria Angula and Aitor Chavarri, narrated by Morgan Ayre.
items.content: As Colombian voters head to the polls to elect their new president, Abelardo de la Esperiella is gaining momentum. Facing the left's Ivan Cepeda, the far-right candidate promises a radical change for the country and assures voters he will restore security with an iron fist. He was promoting his rum and wine businesses before launching his presidential bid. Report by Maria Angula and Aitor Chavarri, narrated by Morgan Ayre.

items.title: Colombians vote in presidential runoff between Trump-backed hardliner, leftist senator
items.standfirst: Voters in Colombia will choose a new president Sunday as hard-right White House-backed lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella faces leftist senator Ivan Cepeda in a high-stakes vote that will decide the fate of the country's stumbling peace process and strained ties with Washington.
items.content: Voters in Colombia will choose a new president Sunday as hard-right White House-backed lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella faces leftist senator Ivan Cepeda in a high-stakes vote that will decide the fate of the country's stumbling peace process and strained ties with Washington.

items.title: Russia pauses fuel sales as Ukrainian strikes kill several, wound dozens in Crimea
items.standfirst: A massive Ukrainian barrage in Russian-occupied Crimea killed at least four people and wounded dozens, Moscow-backed authorities there said Sunday, adding that fuel sales at Crimean petrol stations were suspended. Ukraine said it targeted military and energy facilities in Crimea – Moscow's key logistics base for its four-year military offensive – in one of the biggest attacks on the peninsula in recent months.
items.content: A massive Ukrainian barrage in Russian-occupied Crimea killed at least four people and wounded dozens, Moscow-backed authorities there said Sunday, adding that fuel sales at Crimean petrol stations were suspended. Ukraine said it targeted military and energy facilities in Crimea – Moscow's key logistics base for its four-year military offensive – in one of the biggest attacks on the peninsula in recent months.

clusters.title: Bolivia deploys army to halt protests after declaration of state of emergency
clusters.neutral_headline: Bolivian president declares state of emergency
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, Guardian World, BBC World

items.title: Bolivia deploys army to halt protests after declaration of state of emergency
items.standfirst: Bolivia's government deployed soldiers and bulldozers to raze anti-government roadblocks. This comes after President Rodrigo Paz declared a state of emergency, which curbs the right to protest and allows the president to deploy the military. For more than six weeks, unions, Indigenous groups and coca farmers have protested against economic reforms and blocked roads.
items.content: Bolivia's government deployed soldiers and bulldozers to raze anti-government roadblocks. This comes after President Rodrigo Paz declared a state of emergency, which curbs the right to protest and allows the president to deploy the military. For more than six weeks, unions, Indigenous groups and coca farmers have protested against economic reforms and blocked roads.

Continue reading...

items.title: Los Angeles declares state of emergency as firefighters battle warehouse blaze
items.standfirst: Fire at cold-storage facility that started on 17 June continues to spew smoke across the metro areaMayor Karen Bass has declared a state of emergency for the city of Los Angeles, as firefighters still struggle to contain a blaze from a cold-storage facility that continues to spew smoke across the metro area.“This emergency declaration is crucial because Boyle Heights is not just responding to a fire. Residents have lived through days of smoke, shelter-in-place orders, disruptions to daily life, and ongoing questions about what this means for their health and well-being,” Councilmember Ysabel Jurado, who represents Boyle Heights, said in a statement. Continue reading...

clusters.neutral_headline: Ueda’s brace helps Japan knock Tunisia out of World Cup 2026
bullet_summary: Reported by Al Jazeera, France24

items.title: Ueda’s brace helps Japan knock Tunisia out of World Cup 2026
items.standfirst: Two goals from Ayase Ueda, and one each from Daichi Kamada and Junya Ito, keep Asian giants Japan second in Group F.
items.content: Two goals from Ayase Ueda, and one each from Daichi Kamada and Junya Ito, keep Asian giants Japan second in Group F.

items.title: Ueda shines as Japan knocks out Tunisia in World Cup's 1,000th game
items.standfirst: The World Cup marked its 1000th game with Japan thrashing Tunisia 4-0 on Saturday to close in on a place in the last 32. Ayase Ueda scored twice while Daichi Kamada and Junya Ito were also on target as the Asian giants joined the Netherlands on four points at the top of Group F.
items.content: The World Cup marked its 1000th game with Japan thrashing Tunisia 4-0 on Saturday to close in on a place in the last 32. Ayase Ueda scored twice while Daichi Kamada and Junya Ito were also on target as the Asian giants joined the Netherlands on four points at the top of Group F.

items.content: Ukrainian leader Volodymyr Zelenskyy has returned a state honor to Poland after the Polish president revoked it.

clusters.title: Opinion: Algae doesn't care about our party lines
clusters.neutral_headline: Opinion: Algae doesn't care about our party lines
bullet_summary: Reported by NPR News, CBS News

items.title: Opinion: Algae doesn't care about our party lines
items.standfirst: President Trump's beautification project of the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool has become plagued with a robust algae bloom, despite a $14 million investment and a coating of "American flag blue."
items.content: President Trump's beautification project of the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool has become plagued with a robust algae bloom, despite a $14 million investment and a coating of "American flag blue."(Image credit: Mark Schiefelbein)

items.title: Rips appear in Reflecting Pool's new sealant after algae bloom turn water green
items.standfirst: President Trump's efforts to spruce up the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool faced a snag this week, with algae turning the water green while rips appeared in an "American Flag Blue" surface picked by the president.
items.content: President Trump's efforts to spruce up the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool faced a snag this week, with algae turning the water green while rips appeared in an "American Flag Blue" surface picked by the president.

clusters.title: Scientists Invent a Way to Brew Espresso With Ultrasonic Waves—No Hot Water Required
clusters.neutral_headline: Sound waves made this espresso—and could cut brewing energy use by 75%
bullet_summary: Reported by Wired, Reuters Top News

items.title: Scientists Invent a Way to Brew Espresso With Ultrasonic Waves—No Hot Water Required
items.standfirst: Researchers have demonstrated they can make coffee comparable to conventional espresso using ultrasonic waves. Because the process doesn’t need hot water, it consumes 75 percent less energy.
items.content: Researchers have demonstrated they can make coffee comparable to conventional espresso using ultrasonic waves. Because the process doesn’t need hot water, it consumes 75 percent less energy.

items.title: Sound waves made this espresso—and could cut brewing energy use by 75%
items.standfirst: Ultrasonic espresso uses cold water and high-frequency sound waves to extract coffee with far less energy than conventional brewing. Early taste tests suggest it can match the traditional version. [theconversation.com]
items.content: Ultrasonic espresso uses cold water and high-frequency sound waves to extract coffee with far less energy than conventional brewing. Early taste tests suggest it can match the traditional version. [theconversation.com]

items.title: Trump unveils new converted Air Force One jet he accepted as gift from Qatar

items.content: Donald Trump has unveiled the new Air Force One, an upgraded Boeing 747 gifted by Qatar that has been converted into the official US presidential aircraft.

items.content: "This is considered the world's most luxurious plane," the president said in front of the enormous new jet.

clusters.title: African and Caribbean nations call for formal apology for transatlantic slavery
clusters.neutral_headline: African and Caribbean nations call for formal apology for transatlantic slavery
bullet_summary: Reported by BBC World, Guardian World

items.title: African and Caribbean nations call for formal apology for transatlantic slavery
items.standfirst: The leaders have asked for apologies from the countries that benefited from the slave trade, as well as debt relief and financial compensation.
items.content: The leaders have asked for apologies from the countries that benefited from the slave trade, as well as debt relief and financial compensation.

items.title: Global framework for reparatory justice adopted at landmark Ghana conference
items.standfirst: Call for formal apologies from countries that benefited from transatlantic trade slave included in 19-point roadmapMore than money: the logic of slavery reparationsA global framework for reparatory justice has been adopted at a conference in Ghana, as African and Caribbean leaders demanded formal apologies from countries that benefited from the transatlantic slave trade.Heads of state and government and other officials formally approved the strategy on Friday at a gathering in a hotel in the capital, Accra, which was the first major meeting since the adoption of the landmark United Nations (UN) resolution declaring the trafficking of enslaved Africans as the gravest crime against humanity. Continue reading...
